user_group: Commercial vessels; pilots; port users; recreational boaters
decision_supported: Vessel movement; cargo loading; grounding avoidance; spill response
economic_pathway: Avoided costs; increased loading efficiency; reduced delays; improved spill response
metric: Estimated $2.4M-$4.8M in high-confidence direct annual benefits and $4.4M-$7.0M in presently realized quantifiable annual benefit
limitations: One-time case study; results should not be generalized nationally without method adjustment.
claim_allowed: A NOAA-sponsored Tampa Bay case study estimated measurable annual benefits from PORTS® data.
update_frequency: One-time case study; check for updated valuation
ai_extraction_notes: Extract benefit categories separately; do not generalize nationally without method.

economic_pathway: Increased cargo loading; avoided costs; direct income
metric: Reported $6.4M estimated annual benefit from Lower Columbia River PORTS; separate study notes about $2.8M annual potential benefit for dry bulk loading decisions
limitations: Needs source verification against original PORTS valuation report; current source appears to be a secondary compilation/report.
claim_allowed: Lower Columbia River evidence suggests PORTS® can support loading/navigation decisions with measurable economic benefits, pending source verification.
ai_extraction_notes: Follow up with full PDF/table values and assumptions.

user_group: NOAA; port authorities; vessel operators; policymakers
decision_supported: National investment and port coverage decisions
economic_pathway: Safety and efficiency benefits from expanded real-time navigation information
metric: Estimated as much as $300M annual benefit from expanded PORTS® serving 175 major seaports; potential 10-year NPV up to $2.5B; nearly 60% reduction in groundings at some PORTS-served seaports
limitations: Modeled/scenario estimate; not current realized annual IOOS benefit; assumptions need review before use.
claim_allowed: NOAA estimated large potential benefits from expanded PORTS® coverage, based on case-study evidence and modeling.
ai_extraction_notes: Flag as modeled scenario, not current realized annual IOOS benefit.

user_group: Mariners; pilots; port authorities; coastal managers
decision_supported: Safe navigation; port timing; coastal resource management
economic_pathway: Reduced risk and improved operational efficiency
metric: Not quantified in source; PORTS collects water levels currents salinity bridge air gap and meteorological parameters
limitations: Operational source documents decision-support capability but does not quantify economic value.
claim_allowed: PORTS® is a decision-support system for safe and efficient maritime commerce.
ai_extraction_notes: Extract active systems sensor types users and whether an economic study exists.

region: Florida; Texas; Gulf of Maine; Lake Erie; Pacific Northwest partner forecasts; other regions
user_group: Health officials; shellfish managers; water treatment operators; seafood/tourism sectors
decision_supported: Sampling; beach advisories; shellfish closures; water treatment timing
economic_pathway: Reduced false alarms; targeted monitoring; avoided illness; reduced unnecessary closures
metric: Forecasts can be short-term once or twice weekly; economic value not quantified in source
limitations: Source supports forecast function and decision relevance but not quantified economic value.
claim_allowed: HAB forecasts help managers focus testing and guide closure/advisory decisions.
ai_extraction_notes: Extract region forecast frequency manager decision and closure/advisory link.

user_group: States; local managers; shellfisheries; water treatment facilities
decision_supported: Where/when to sample; when to close/reopen areas
economic_pathway: Lower monitoring costs; fewer false alarms; earlier harvest before closures
metric: Source says forecasts target sampling save localities money help shellfisheries stay open or harvest earlier; dollar value not quantified
limitations: Economic pathway is described qualitatively; no dollar estimate in source.
claim_allowed: HAB forecasts can reduce unnecessary response costs and support more targeted closures where operational use is documented.
ai_extraction_notes: Good candidate for follow-up with state closure datasets.

user_group: Shellfish managers; recreational harvesters; Tribal users; coastal businesses
economic_pathway: Lost recreational spending and harvest access
metric: Year-long domoic acid closures; estimated $10M-$12M reduced recreational spending for most recent closure
limitations: Not IOOS-specific; use as evidence of HAB economic consequences, not IOOS benefit.
claim_allowed: HAB-related shellfish closures can have measurable local economic impacts.
ai_extraction_notes: Use as exposure/economic consequence; do not frame as IOOS-attributable benefit.

decision_supported: Shellfish closure and public health response
economic_pathway: Better closure timing and community response
metric: Data described as crucial in developing community response and state/federal shellfish closures during historic 2005 PSP event; economic value not quantified
limitations: Source supports decision relevance but not quantified economic value; needs state closure and economic records for stronger claim.
claim_allowed: Regional observing data supported shellfish closure and response decisions during a major HAB event.
ai_extraction_notes: Follow up with closure duration affected acreage shellfish value and state agency records.

ioos_component: IOOS HF radar surface currents + USCG SAROPS
decision_supported: Drift prediction; search area planning; rescue asset allocation
economic_pathway: Reduced search area; improved probability of success; lower sortie time/cost; lives/property saved
metric: Not quantified in source; IOOS provides gridded HF radar data and SAROPS uses environmental data for maritime search planning
limitations: Clear operational pathway but no quantified economic outcome in source.
claim_allowed: HF radar surface-current data support USCG search planning through SAROPS.
ai_extraction_notes: Need case-level SAR records to quantify time sorties lives or property saved.

decision_supported: Search area reduction and SAR planning
economic_pathway: Increased search efficiency and response effectiveness
metric: Source says integration of HF radar current data into SAROPS reduces search areas and increases search effectiveness; no numeric value provided
limitations: Regional product page supports decision pathway but does not quantify economic outcomes.
claim_allowed: Regional HF radar data can improve SAR planning by reducing search areas.
ai_extraction_notes: Follow up with USCG district examples and SAROPS case documentation.

ioos_component: IOOS Pacific Region OA Data Portal / Burkolator stations
economic_pathway: Avoided larval losses; reduced wasted effort; protected hatchery production
metric: Portal delivers real-time OA data streams; project deployed new Burkolator stations at Carlsbad Aquafarm Hog Island Oyster Co. and Alutiiq Pride supplementing Whiskey Creek and Taylor Shellfish sites
limitations: Source documents monitoring infrastructure and users but not quantified economic outcomes.
claim_allowed: IOOS-supported OA monitoring provides real-time information used by shellfish growers and hatcheries.
ai_extraction_notes: Extract station operator parameter and documented management decision.

decision_supported: Schedule production; buffer/adjust intake water; avoid poor water-quality periods
economic_pathway: Reduced hatchery mortality risk and wasted resources
metric: West Coast shellfish described as a $111M industry; monitoring gives 1-2 day early warning of acidified seawater and helps managers schedule or adjust production
limitations: Industry value is exposure/context; source does not quantify IOOS-attributable avoided losses.
claim_allowed: OA observations support hatchery decisions that reduce operational risk.
ai_extraction_notes: Strong follow-up row if hatchery outcomes or cost savings can be documented.

user_group: NOAA; IOOS; coastal partners; shellfish hatcheries
decision_supported: Early warning and adaptation to acidified seawater
economic_pathway: Protected production timing and water treatment decisions
metric: NOAA Fisheries states OA nearly collapsed the $117M West Coast shellfish industry; NOAA/IOOS data enabled early warning for acidified seawater and hatchery scheduling/intake decisions
limitations: $117M is industry exposure/risk context, not quantified IOOS savings; wording must avoid implying IOOS saved the full industry value.
claim_allowed: NOAA and IOOS observations helped coastal partners develop early warning information for hatchery management.
ai_extraction_notes: Avoid wording 'IOOS saved $117M'; source describes industry at risk, not quantified IOOS savings.

user_group: Ocean observing/data businesses; NOAA; policymakers; investors
decision_supported: Industry assessment; workforce/market planning; public-private engagement
metric: 2020 survey identified 814 businesses about 325000 employees and estimated $8B Ocean Enterprise revenue; 22% of revenue from exports
limitations: Strong for Ocean Enterprise sector scale; not direct evidence that IOOS caused all revenue or jobs.
claim_allowed: The Ocean Enterprise is a measurable business cluster tied to ocean observing and information services.
update_frequency: Periodic study; annual proxy possible via QCEW/CBP
ai_extraction_notes: Do not claim IOOS created all revenue/jobs; extract definitions and survey method.

region: Atlantic basin; Caribbean; Gulf; Mid-Atlantic; South Atlantic
user_group: NOAA; NWS; Navy; emergency managers; modelers
decision_supported: Hurricane intensity modeling and forecast support
economic_pathway: Better forecast inputs; improved preparedness; risk reduction
metric: Not quantified in source; glider data are delivered to global ocean circulation models and realistic ocean conditions help produce more accurate hurricane intensity forecasts
limitations: Clear forecast-support role but no economic valuation or avoided-loss attribution in source.
claim_allowed: IOOS-coordinated glider observations support hurricane intensity forecasting.
ai_extraction_notes: Need forecast verification and avoided-loss studies before economic claims.

region: U.S. coasts; Puerto Rico; U.S. Virgin Islands; Hawaii; select Pacific islands; southeast Alaska
user_group: Emergency managers; NWS; coastal planners; public
decision_supported: Flood monitoring; warning awareness; threshold-based response
economic_pathway: Better preparedness; road/facility closure planning; reduced uncertainty
metric: Not quantified in source; dashboard uses NOAA water-level stations and provides current/historical inundation information; NWLON has over 200 long-term water-level stations
claim_allowed: NOAA water-level observations support coastal inundation monitoring and local flood decision-making.
ai_extraction_notes: Extract station threshold flood advisory and local decision; avoid avoided-damage claims without BCA.

decision_supported: Locate/apply coastal data and tools for resilience planning
economic_pathway: Time savings; better planning; reduced data access costs
metric: Estimated annual net benefits grew from $2.3M in 2009 to more than $6.7M in 2013; projected 15-year net benefit $117M and ROI 411%
limitations: Strong analogous valuation for coastal data infrastructure, but not IOOS-specific.
claim_allowed: Related coastal data infrastructure can produce measurable planning and access benefits.
ai_extraction_notes: Use as analogous evidence for public data infrastructure value, not direct IOOS impact.
